chunks.en: I usually do my grocery shopping at the supermarket near my station about twice a week.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい週2回くらい、駅の近くのスーパーで食料品の買い物をします。

chunks.en: I decide what to buy by checking my fridge first and jotting down a quick list.
chunks.ja: 何を買うかは、まず冷蔵庫をチェックして、さっとリストを書いて決めます。

chunks.en: At the checkout, I pay with my phone and bag everything up myself pretty fast.
chunks.ja: レジではスマホで支払いをして、自分でわりと手早く袋詰めをします。

chunks.en: The handiest thing is the self-checkout, but the long queues at weekends really drive me crazy.
chunks.ja: 一番便利なのはセルフレジですが、週末の長い行列には本当にうんざりします。

outline: Opening: where and how often I shop
Point 1: how I plan what to buy
Point 2: paying and bagging at checkout
Wrap-up: one handy and one annoying thing

modelTalk.text: Let me tell you how I usually handle my grocery shopping. I pop into the supermarket near my flat about twice a week. Before I head out, I always check the fridge to see what's running low. Then I jot down a quick list on my phone, so I don't forget the basics. Honestly, if I shopped without a list, I'd end up buying loads of junk. At the checkout, I tap my card because it's faster than fumbling for cash. I bring my own bags now, and I bag everything myself while the cashier scans. The handy thing is the self-checkout, which saves me from long queues. What really drives me up the wall, though, is when they run out of something I need. When that happens, I either grab a substitute or pop back the next day. All in all, a little planning keeps the whole thing painless.

chunks.en: To be honest, I usually do my grocery shopping at the same supermarket near my station about twice a week.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、私はたいてい駅の近くの同じスーパーで週に2回ほど食料品の買い物をしています。

chunks.en: When it comes to deciding what to buy, I quickly check the fridge and jot down what's running low.
chunks.ja: 何を買うか決めるときは、冷蔵庫をさっと確認して、切れかけているものをメモします。

chunks.en: At the checkout, I usually pay with my phone and bag everything myself at the packing counter.
chunks.ja: レジでは、たいていスマホで支払って、袋詰めカウンターで自分で全部詰めています。

chunks.en: The one thing that really bugs me is queuing for ages when only one register is open.
chunks.ja: 一つ本当に面倒なのは、レジが一つしか開いていなくて長時間並ばされることです。

outline: Opening: where and how often I shop
Point 1: how I decide what to buy
Point 2: paying and bagging at checkout
Wrap-up: the annoying part about it

modelTalk.text:
So, when it comes to groceries, I usually pop into the same supermarket just around the corner. I go maybe twice a week, mostly on my way home. To be honest, I hate doing one massive shop. Before I head out, I always peek in the fridge to see what's running low. Then I jot down a quick list on my phone, so I don't forget the basics. That said, I still end up grabbing bits I don't really need.

At the checkout, I usually tap my card — it's quicker than fumbling for cash. The annoying part? I've got to bag everything myself, and there's this pressure to be fast. So I try to pack the heavy stuff first, then the fragile things on top.

What I actually love is the self-checkout. When the queues are massive, it's a real lifesaver. Plus, nobody's watching what I buy, which, honestly, suits me just fine.
